NKJV.1: A Michtam of David. Preserve me, O God, for in You I put my trust.
NKJV.2: O my soul, you have said to the LORD, “You are my Lord, My goodness is nothing apart from You.”
NKJV.3: As for the saints who are on the earth, “They are the excellent ones, in whom is all my delight.”
NKJV.4: Their sorrows shall be multiplied who hasten after another god; Their drink offerings of blood I will not offer, Nor take up their names on my lips.
NKJV.5: O LORD, You are the portion of my inheritance and my cup; You maintain my lot.
NKJV.6: The lines have fallen to me in pleasant places; Yes, I have a good inheritance.
NKJV.7: I will bless the LORD who has given me counsel; My heart also instructs me in the night seasons.
NKJV.8: I have set the LORD always before me; Because He is at my right hand I shall not be moved.
NKJV.9: Therefore my heart is glad, and my glory rejoices; My flesh also will rest in hope.
NKJV.10: For You will not leave my soul in Sheol, Nor will You allow Your Holy One to see corruption.
NKJV.11: You will show me the path of life; In Your presence is fullness of joy; At Your right hand are pleasures forevermore.

KJV.5: The LORD<S>3068</S> is the portion<S>4490</S> of mine inheritance<S>2506</S> and of my cup<S>3563</S>: thou maintainest<S>8551</S> my lot<S>1486</S>. <sup>of mine: Heb. of my part</sup>

KJV.7: I will bless<S>1288</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, who hath given me counsel<S>3289</S>: my reins<S>3629</S> also instruct<S>3256</S> me in the night seasons<S>3915</S>.

ESV.1: Preserve me, O God, for in you I take refuge.
ESV.2: I say to the LORD, “You are my Lord; I have no good apart from you.”
ESV.3: As for the saints in the land, they are the excellent ones, in whom is all my delight.
ESV.4: The sorrows of those who run after  another god shall multiply; their drink offerings of blood I will not pour out or take their names on my lips.
ESV.5: The LORD is my chosen portion and my cup; you hold my lot.
ESV.6: The lines have fallen for me in pleasant places; indeed, I have a beautiful inheritance.
ESV.7: I bless the LORD who gives me counsel; in the night also my heart instructs me.
ESV.8: I have set the LORD always before me; because he is at my right hand, I shall not be shaken.
ESV.9: Therefore my heart is glad, and my whole being  rejoices; my flesh also dwells secure.
ESV.10: For you will not abandon my soul to Sheol, or let your holy one see corruption.
ESV.11: You make known to me the path of life; in your presence there is fullness of joy; at your right hand are pleasures forevermore.

NASB.1: A Mikhtam of David. Preserve me, O God, for I take refuge in You.
NASB.2: I said to the LORD, 'You are my Lord; I have no good besides You.'
NASB.3: As for the saints who are in the earth, They are the majestic ones in whom is all my delight.
NASB.4: The sorrows of those who have bartered for another [god] will be multiplied; I shall not pour out their drink offerings of blood, Nor will I take their names upon my lips.
NASB.5: The LORD is the portion of my inheritance and my cup; You support my lot.
NASB.6: The lines have fallen to me in pleasant places; Indeed, my heritage is beautiful to me.
NASB.7: I will bless the LORD who has counseled me; Indeed, my mind instructs me in the night.
NASB.8: I have set the LORD continually before me; Because He is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.
NASB.9: Therefore my heart is glad and my glory rejoices; My flesh also will dwell securely.
NASB.10: For You will not abandon my soul to Sheol; Nor will You allow Your Holy One to undergo decay.
NASB.11: You will make known to me the path of life; In Your presence is fullness of joy; In Your right hand there are pleasures forever.

NLT.1:
Keep me safe, O God,
for I have come to you for refuge.
NLT.2:
I said to the LORD, “You are my Master!
Every good thing I have comes from you.”
NLT.3:
The godly people in the land
are my true heroes!
I take pleasure in them!
NLT.4:
Troubles multiply for those who chase after other gods.
I will not take part in their sacrifices of blood
or even speak the names of their gods.
NLT.5:
LORD, you alone are my inheritance, my cup of blessing.
You guard all that is mine.
NLT.6:
The land you have given me is a pleasant land.
What a wonderful inheritance!
NLT.7:
I will bless the LORD who guides me;
even at night my heart instructs me.
NLT.8:
I know the LORD is always with me.
I will not be shaken, for he is right beside me.
NLT.9:
No wonder my heart is glad, and I rejoice.
My body rests in safety.
NLT.10:
For you will not leave my soul among the dead
or allow your holy one to rot in the grave.
NLT.11:
You will show me the way of life,
granting me the joy of your presence
and the pleasures of living with you forever.

WEB.1: A Poem by David. Preserve me, God, for in you do I take refuge.
WEB.2: My soul, you have said to Yahweh, “You are my Lord. Apart from you I have no good thing.”
WEB.3: As for the saints who are in the earth, they are the excellent ones in whom is all my delight.
WEB.4: Their sorrows shall be multiplied who give gifts to another god. Their drink offerings of blood I will not offer, nor take their names on my lips.
WEB.5: Yahweh assigned my portion and my cup. You made my lot secure.
WEB.6: The lines have fallen to me in pleasant places. Yes, I have a good inheritance.
WEB.7: I will bless Yahweh, who has given me counsel. Yes, my heart instructs me in the night seasons.
WEB.8: I have set Yahweh always before me. Because he is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.
WEB.9: Therefore my heart is glad, and my tongue rejoices. My body shall also dwell in safety.
WEB.10: For you will not leave my soul in Sheol,  neither will you allow your holy one to see corruption.
WEB.11: You will show me the path of life. In your presence is fullness of joy. In your right hand there are pleasures forever more.

CSB17.1: A of David. ⓓ [1] Protect me, God, for I take refuge in you. ⓔ
CSB17.2:
I [1] said to the LORD, “You are my Lord;
I have nothing good besides you.” [2] ⓕ
CSB17.3:
As for the holy people who are in the land, ⓖ
they are the noble ones.
All my delight is in them.
CSB17.4:
The sorrows of those who take another god
for themselves will multiply;
I will not pour out their drink offerings of blood,
and I will not speak their names with my lips. ⓗ
CSB17.5:
LORD, you are my portion [3]
and my cup of blessing;
you hold my future. ⓘ
CSB17.6:
The boundary lines have fallen for me
in pleasant places;
indeed, I have a beautiful inheritance. ⓙ
CSB17.7:
I will bless the LORD who counsels me ⓚ  —
even at night when my thoughts trouble me. [4] ⓛ
CSB17.8:
I always let the LORD guide me. [5]
Because he is at my right hand,

Therefore my heart is glad
and my whole being rejoices;

For you will not abandon me to Sheol; ⓐ

CSB17.11:
You reveal the path of life to me;
in your presence is abundant joy; ⓒ

AMP.1: A Mikhtam of David [probably intended to record memorable thoughts]. Keep and protect me, O God, for in You I have placed my trust and found refuge.
AMP.2:
I said to the LORD, "You are my Lord;
I have no good besides You."
AMP.3:
As for the saints (godly people) who are in the land,
They are the majestic and the noble and the excellent ones in whom is all my delight.
AMP.4:
The sorrows [pain and suffering] of those who have chosen another god will be multiplied [because of their idolatry];
I will not pour out their drink offerings of blood,
Nor will I take their names upon my lips.
AMP.5:
The LORD is the portion of my inheritance, my cup [He is all I need];
You support my lot.
AMP.6:
The [boundary] lines [of the land] have fallen for me in pleasant places;
Indeed, my heritage is beautiful to me.
AMP.7:
I will bless the LORD who has counseled me;
Indeed, my heart (mind) instructs me in the night.
AMP.8:
I have set the LORD continually before me;
Because He is at my right hand, I will not be shaken.
AMP.9:
Therefore my heart is glad and my glory [my innermost self] rejoices;
My body too will dwell [confidently] in safety,
AMP.10:
For You will not abandon me to Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead),
Nor will You allow Your Holy One to undergo decay.
AMP.11:
You will show me the path of life;
In Your presence is fullness of joy;
In Your right hand there are pleasures forevermore.

MSG.1:
A David song
Keep me safe, O God,
I've run for dear life to you.
MSG.2:
I say to God, "Be my Lord!"
Without you, nothing makes sense.
MSG.3:
And these God-chosen lives all around—
what splendid friends they make!
MSG.4:
Don't just go shopping for a god.
Gods are not for sale.
I swear I'll never treat god-names
like brand-names.
MSG.5:
My choice is you, God, first and only.
And now I find I'm your choice!
MSG.6:
You set me up with a house and yard.
And then you made me your heir!
MSG.7:
The wise counsel God gives when I'm awake
is confirmed by my sleeping heart.
MSG.8:
Day and night I'll stick with God;
I've got a good thing going and I'm not letting go.
MSG.9:
I'm happy from the inside out,
and from the outside in, I'm firmly formed.
MSG.10:
You canceled my ticket to hell—
that's not my destination!
MSG.11:
Now you've got my feet on the life path,
all radiant from the shining of your face.
Ever since you took my hand,
I'm on the right way.

NIV.1:
Psalm 16
A miktam of David.
Keep me safe, O God,
for in you I take refuge.
NIV.2:
I said to the Lord, “You are my Lord;
apart from you I have no good thing.”
NIV.3:
As for the saints who are in the land,
they are the glorious ones in whom is all my delight.
NIV.4:
The sorrows of those will increase
who run after other gods.
I will not pour out their libations of blood
or take up their names on my lips.
NIV.5:
Lord, you have assigned me my portion and my cup;
you have made my lot secure.
NIV.6:
The boundary lines have fallen for me in pleasant places;
surely I have a delightful inheritance.
NIV.7:
I will praise the Lord, who counsels me;
even at night my heart instructs me.
NIV.8:
I have set the Lord always before me.
Because he is at my right hand,
I will not be shaken.
NIV.9:
Therefore my heart is glad and my tongue rejoices;
my body also will rest secure,
NIV.10:
because you will not abandon me to the grave,
nor will you let your Holy One see decay.
NIV.11:
You have made known to me the path of life;
you will fill me with joy in your presence,
with eternal pleasures at your right hand.

NRSVCE.1: Protect me, O God, for in you I take refuge.
NRSVCE.2:
I say to the LORD, “You are my Lord;
I have no good apart from you.”
NRSVCE.3:
As for the holy ones in the land, they are the noble,
in whom is all my delight.
NRSVCE.4:
Those who choose another god multiply their sorrows;
their drink offerings of blood I will not pour out
or take their names upon my lips.
NRSVCE.5:
The LORD is my chosen portion and my cup;
you hold my lot.
NRSVCE.6:
The boundary lines have fallen for me in pleasant places;
I have a goodly heritage.
NRSVCE.7:
I bless the LORD who gives me counsel;
in the night also my heart instructs me.
NRSVCE.8:
I keep the LORD always before me;
because he is at my right hand, I shall not be moved.
NRSVCE.9:
Therefore my heart is glad, and my soul rejoices;
my body also rests secure.
NRSVCE.10:
For you do not give me up to Sheol,
or let your faithful one see the Pit.
NRSVCE.11:
You show me the path of life.
In your presence there is fullness of joy;
in your right hand are pleasures forevermore.

NET.1: Protect me, O God, for I have taken shelter in you.
NET.2: I say to the LORD, “You are the Lord, my only source of well-being.”
NET.3: As for God’s chosen people who are in the land, and the leading officials I admired so much —
NET.4: their troubles multiply, they desire other gods. I will not pour out drink offerings of blood to their gods, nor will I make vows in the name of their gods.
NET.5: LORD, you give me stability and prosperity; you make my future secure.
NET.6: It is as if I have been given fertile fields or received a beautiful tract of land.
NET.7: I will praise the LORD who guides me; yes, during the night I reflect and learn.
NET.8: I constantly trust in the LORD; because he is at my right hand, I will not be upended.
NET.9: So my heart rejoices and I am happy; My life is safe.
NET.10: You will not abandon me to Sheol; you will not allow your faithful follower to see the Pit.
NET.11: You lead me in the path of life; I experience absolute joy in your presence; you always give me sheer delight.
